channel.title: My AI Filtered News
channel.description: Cleaned news feed via Gemini AI

item.title: WATCH LIVE: Artemis II astronauts have splashed down on Earth
item.description: The four astronauts aboard NASA's Artemis II mission are about to plunge through the atmosphere toward Earth, after a successful visit to the moon.

item.title: Colombia responds to Ecuador’s tariff hike with 100-percent import tax
item.description: The tit-for-tat tariff increase comes amid cross-border tension over illicit drugs and fate of politician Jorge Glas.

item.title: Brazil announces US partnership to intercept weapons, drug trafficking
item.description: President Lula's government said it has seized 1,168 illicit arms imported from the US in the last 12 months alone.

item.title: Israeli strike on government building kills Lebanese officers
item.description: Israeli air strike on a government building in southern Lebanon’s Nabatieh kills at least 13.

item.title: Iran war live: US negotiators due to arrive in Pakistan for ceasefire talks
item.description: Lebanese Health Ministry says people killed in Israeli attacks since March 2 rises to 1,953 with 6,303 wounded.

item.title: Artemis II splashdown: Astronauts return to Earth after lunar mission
item.description: Artemis II crew landed in the Pacific Ocean under parachutes after a high-speed re-entry that tested its heat shield.

item.title: Fed seeks details on U.S. banks’ exposure to private credit firms
item.description: The Treasury Department is also questioning the insurance industry about exposures to private credit.

item.title: Tiger Global Backs PopUp Bagels at $300 Million Valuation
item.description: Tiger Global has agreed to invest in PopUp Bagels at a $300 million valuation, according to people familiar with the matter — roughly five times what the startup was quoted at as recently as five months ago.

item.title: Wall Street Week | Iran Oil Fallout
item.description: This week, Richard Haass of Centerview Partners on what to expect out of US-Iran peace talks. Plus, why Gulf states are begging Trump not to end the Iran war early and how the conflict could hit consumers, from gas prices to interest rates. And, former Canadian Finance Minister Chrystia Freeland warns of stagflation, a food crisis, and the growing strain on the US-led global order as finance leaders gather in Washington at the annual World Bank-IMF meetings. Later, how China quietly built a monopoly on the minerals that power modern life, and why American innovators think they can still leapfrog Beijing. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: California: Eric Swalwell staffers quit after new sexual assault allegations
item.description:
Allies of Democratic governor contender withdraw support after accusations in San Francisco Chronicle and on CNN

California gubernatorial candidate Eric Swalwell’s campaign suffered a major blow on Friday as several staffers resigned and prominent political leaders withdrew their support in the wake of multiple allegations of past sexual misconduct.

The San Francisco Chronicle published an account of a woman who said she was sexually assaulted by Swalwell on two separate occasions.

Continue reading...

item.title: Calls mount for California governor candidate Eric Swalwell to quit after multiple women accuse him of sexual assault – live
item.description:
An ex-staffer of Swalwell and three other women allege sexual assault or misconduct in news reports as the US representative’s gubernatorial campaign implodes

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US email

On Truth Social, Donald Trump issued a cryptic message this morning that appeared to be in reference to the upcoming negotiations in Islamabad, but remains unclear.

“WORLD’S MOST POWERFUL RESET!!!” he wrote.

Continue reading...
